source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - WD 32 E Platform Oil Discharge

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Oil Discharge to Gulf Waters
actions_taken: BSEE Inspectors directed personnel to immediately shut the platform in and stop the source of the oil discharge
Personnel In Charge (PIC) directed to report the pollution incident to the USCG
LSH 1 (Fisher 2100) shut down device installed and function tested in the oil chamber of the floatation cell
BSEE issued E-100 Incident of Non-Compliance for pollution event
BSEE issued additional E-104 Incident of Non-Compliance
event.summary: On 21 September 2016 at 0828 hours, BSEE Inspectors discovered an oil sheen 7 miles long and 1 mile wide originating from WD 32 E Platform. Investigation revealed that the Wet Oil Transfer Pump (PBA-410E) failed due to a stuck internal pump shifting/slider valve, preventing oil transfer from the Floatation Cell (ABM-410). Oil level in the oil chamber rose to the level of the produced water side and flowed back into the overboard discharge chamber. The Fisher 2500 Level Controller maintained preset water discharge level, causing oil to discharge through the overboard water outlet. The Level Safety High (LSH 1) shut down device was not installed in the oil chamber as required, preventing automatic platform shut-in. Approximately 8.05 barrels of crude oil were discharged into Gulf waters. The unmanned facility was in-service and producing at the time of discovery.
recommendations: LSH 1 (Fisher 2100) shut down device has been installed and function tested in the oil chamber of the floatation cell to prevent carryover of produced oil back into the water chamber and activate system shut-in
BSEE rescinded the 3-times-per-week pollution inspection frequency and now requires daily pollution inspection for WD 32 DAE facility
key_phrases: Level Safety High shut down device not installed
oil carryover into produced water discharge

hazards.name: Produced oil and water mixture in floatation cell
High oil level in floatation cell oil chamber

threats.name: Wet Oil Transfer Pump (PBA-410E) internal failure - stuck shifting/slider valve
Oil level rise in floatation cell oil chamber above water chamber interface
Oil carryover from oil chamber into overboard water discharge chamber
Fisher 2500 Level Controller maintaining preset water level while oil present

consequences.name: Unauthorized discharge of crude oil into Gulf of Mexico waters
Environmental pollution - 8.05 barrels of oil released

controls.name: Wet Oil Transfer Pump (PBA-410E)
controls.barrier_role: Prevent oil accumulation in floatation cell oil chamber by transferring oil to storage
controls.lod_basis: Primary mechanical control to prevent oil level rise

supporting_text: The Wet Oil Transfer Pump (PBA-410E) required to transfer oil from the Floatation Cell's (ABM-410) oil chamber, failed to pump due to a stuck, internal pump shifting/slider valve.

controls.name: Level Safety High (LSH 1) shut down device - Fisher 2100
controls.barrier_role: Detect high oil level in oil chamber and initiate platform shut-in to prevent discharge
controls.lod_basis: Safety instrumented system to prevent loss of containment

supporting_text: The Level Safety High (LSH 1) shut down device for the oil chamber was not installed in the oil chamber as requested by Energy XXI and approved by BSEE.
The LSH 1 shut down device (Fisher 2100) was installed external to the overboard water chamber of the floatation cell and not in the oil chamber as required.

controls.name: Fisher 2500 Overboard Water Level Controller
controls.barrier_role: Maintain preset water level in floatation cell to prevent overflow
controls.lod_basis: Automatic level control to manage discharge

supporting_text: The Fisher 2500 Level Controller, controlling the overboard water dump valve, discharged all fluids (water and oil) to maintain the preset level in the unit.
As the Fisher 2500 Level Controller for the water discharge maintains the preset level, oil was discharging out the overboard water outlet.

controls.name: Wemco Overboard Water Treatment Floatation Cell (ABM-410)
controls.barrier_role: Separate oil from produced water to prevent oil discharge
controls.lod_basis: Primary separation equipment for oil-water treatment

supporting_text: The Inspectors observed the (Wemco) Overboard Water Treatment Floatation Cell (ABM-410), located on the top deck, full of thick, brown, produced oil and dumping the oil out of the overboard water outlet, down to the Gulf Waters below.

controls.name: Pollution Inspection Frequency
controls.barrier_role: Detect and identify pollution events through regular facility inspections
controls.lod_basis: Regulatory oversight and detection of operational anomalies

supporting_text: The WD DAE facility had an approved Pollution Inspection Frequency of 3 times per week; however, due to several reported nuisance overboard water upsets and sheens, and a recalculation of the pollution matrix, BSEE has rescinded the departure and requires a daily pollution inspection.
On 9/15/2016 during a Random BSEE Inspection, a small sheen from the Floatation Cell was observed by the inspectors who then requested the operator to call the NRC

controls.name: Unmanned Facility Operations Monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Monitor unmanned facility operations and detect anomalies

supporting_text: The Inspectors landed and found the facilities to be unmanned at the time of the incident.
WD 32 DAE was an unmanned facility with 4 flowing wells.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
